base_queries: {company_name} M&A appetite acquisitions deal history HR tech
{company_name} competitive moat market position HR HCM
{company_name} earnings call transcript HR revenue growth
{company_name} HR media content community platform acquisition
vertical_queries: HR tech M&A consolidation {company_name} buyer strategic

research_goals: CEO vision: stated strategic direction, platform ambitions, content/community strategy
M&A appetite: confirmed acquisitions in HR/HCM space (name the deals, dates, amounts)
Competitive moat: market share, product differentiation, customer lock-in
Earnings quotes: executive statements on growth, HR segment performance, acquisition rationale
Media business fit: how would this buyer benefit from owning a 27-year HR media platform with 1.8M+ professionals?
Domain value: strategic value of a premium 2-letter .com in the HR namespace
Market reputation: product reviews (G2, Capterra, Trustpilot), NPS signals, customer sentiment
Strategic fit: why this buyer specifically — synergies, gaps filled, competitive positioning
additional_goals: HR product portfolio: which HCM modules does this buyer offer (recruiting, payroll, learning, performance, workforce planning)?
Content strategy: does this buyer already invest in content marketing, events, certifications, or community?
First-party data appetite: does this buyer value audience data, intent signals, engagement data?
Revenue from HR segment: what percentage of total revenue comes from HR/HCM products?
story_hooks: The HR technology market is consolidating at historic speed — and the companies winning aren't just buying software, they're buying audiences.
A 27-year-old media platform with 1.8 million engaged HR professionals isn't a content business — it's a distribution moat that no amount of ad spend can replicate.
The acquirers paying the highest prices in HR tech right now share one thesis: owning the conversation with HR buyers is more valuable than owning another feature.
synthesis_prompts.market_context: HR tech M&A is at a cyclical peak — Paychex acquired Paycor for $4.1B, Thoma Bravo took Dayforce private for $12.3B, SAP acquired SmartRecruiters, and Workday completed three acquisitions in 2025 alone. Strategic buyers are paying premium prices for assets that deliver audience, data, and distribution alongside product.
synthesis_prompts.buyer_appetite: Strategic acquirers (Paychex, Workday, SAP, ADP) and PE platforms (Thoma Bravo, HG Capital) are actively acquiring in the HR tech ecosystem. The convergence of AI, content, and community is creating a new class of valuable assets beyond pure SaaS.
synthesis_prompts.premium_driver: The assets commanding premium multiples in HR tech today combine three things: a large, engaged professional audience (distribution), proprietary first-party data (intent and engagement signals), and trusted content that drives purchasing decisions. Standalone software trades at revenue multiples; software plus audience trades at a structural premium.

buyer_scoring_criteria.score_8_description: Confirmed acquisition in HR tech, HR media, or B2B professional community (must name the deal)
buyer_scoring_criteria.score_6_description: Operates in HR/HCM space, no confirmed media or community acquisition
buyer_scoring_criteria.score_4_description: No HR tech presence, pure financial buyer with no sector thesis

buyer_scoring_criteria.score_8_description: Buyer has strong product but weak content/community/audience — HR.com fills a documented strategic gap
buyer_scoring_criteria.score_6_description: Buyer has some content presence but limited compared to HR.com's 1.8M audience
buyer_scoring_criteria.score_4_description: Buyer already owns comparable audience or has no distribution strategy

buyer_scoring_criteria.score_8_description: Buyer has publicly stated intent-data or audience-data monetization strategy
buyer_scoring_criteria.score_6_description: Buyer collects product usage data but no content/intent layer
buyer_scoring_criteria.score_4_description: No evidence of data monetization strategy

buyer_scoring_criteria.score_8_description: Buyer's confirmed deal range covers $15M-$35M; balance sheet or fund supports it
buyer_scoring_criteria.score_6_description: Buyer could stretch to this range but it would be at the edge of typical deal size
buyer_scoring_criteria.score_4_description: Significant mismatch — buyer does $100M+ deals or sub-$5M deals

buyer_scoring_criteria.score_8_description: Buyer has dedicated corp dev team and completed 2+ deals in past 24 months
buyer_scoring_criteria.score_6_description: Buyer has done acquisitions but infrequently or without dedicated team
buyer_scoring_criteria.score_4_description: No acquisition history or known to be slow/bureaucratic

valuation_fields._comment_primary_metric: TODO: HR media businesses trade on revenue multiples, not EBITDA. Confirm with Ewing whether the three-asset structure (domain + media + My People) should use separate valuation methods.
valuation_fields._comment_multiples: TODO: These are media business multiples only (1.2-1.5x revenue at ~20% EBITDA margins). Domain valued separately at $15-20M via broker comps. My People platform valued separately at $250K-$5M depending on traction. Need domain knowledge: what are current B2B media M&A comps? HubSpot/Hustle, Informa acquisitions, etc.
valuation_fields._comment_margin: ~20% EBITDA margins per meeting notes. TODO: Confirm whether this is trailing or normalized.

growth_levers.description: 1.8M engaged HR professionals generating intent signals (content consumption, cert paths, event attendance) — packageable for HR tech vendors as lead-gen and market intelligence.
30+ research institutes producing original HR research. AI integration can personalize content delivery, automate insights, and scale production without proportional cost increase.
HR certification prep programs create recurring revenue and deep engagement. Expanding cert offerings (SHRM, HRCI, specialty certs) increases LTV per professional.
5,000+ annual webcasts and 200+ virtual events. Converting virtual attendees to premium in-person experiences (HR West conference model) increases per-event revenue.
B2B community tech rebuilt 2023, live June 2025. 35 HR tech clients interested, 7 going live. White-label community platform is a separate growth asset with its own revenue trajectory.
hr.com is one of only 676 two-letter .com domains globally. Ranked #87 for traffic. Domain alone valued at $15-20M and can be sold separately or retained as brand equity.

letter_config._comment_certs: TODO: Not applicable in the traditional sense. The relevant credibility markers are: 27 years in operation, 1.8M professionals, 5,000+ annual webcasts, 30+ research institutes. These should replace certification mentions in letter templates.

fee_structure.engagement_fee_range: TODO: Confirm fee structure with Ewing — home services fees ($5K-$25K) unlikely to apply to a $22-35M three-asset deal
fee_structure.monthly_retainer_range: TODO: Likely higher retainer given deal complexity (three assets, domain broker coordination, potential separate sales)
fee_structure.success_fee_description: TODO: Success fee structure needs to account for three-asset packaging — single fee on bundled sale vs. separate fees per asset

pipeline_prompts.synthesis_context: You are a senior M&A analyst at an advisory firm. Your client is selling HR.com — a media and learning platform with 2M+ HR professionals — to potential acquirers. You are writing buyer intelligence about {buyer_name}.
pipeline_prompts.nuggets_context: You are extracting golden nuggets from RAW search results about {buyer_name} for an M&A advisor selling HR.com.
pipeline_prompts.nuggets_focus: Why {buyer_name} would want to acquire an HR media and learning platform
pipeline_prompts.product_discovery_query: {buyer_name} offers a suite of HR products and platforms that can be individually reviewed, including modules for recruiting, payroll, learning, and performance management

pipeline_sections.title: HR.com Media Business
pipeline_sections.topic_template: {buyer_name} operates in the HR technology space and could benefit from acquiring HR.com's media audience of 2 million HR professionals, learning marketplace, and advertising revenue

pipeline_sections.title: HR.com Domain Name Value
pipeline_sections.topic_template: HR media, content, and domain authority that would give {buyer_name} a premium brand presence and SEO advantage in the HR technology market

pipeline_sections.title: Market Reputation
pipeline_sections.topic_template: {buyer_name} customer satisfaction, product reviews, and market reputation that would affect their ability to integrate an acquired HR media platform

pipeline_sections.title: Strategic Fit
pipeline_sections.topic_template: HR media, content, and learning businesses that would complement {buyer_name}'s existing platform and strengthen their position in the HR technology market

pipeline_sections.title: CEO Vision & Strategy
pipeline_sections.topic_template: {buyer_name} CEO has signaled interest in acquiring content, media, or learning platforms to expand their HR technology ecosystem

pipeline_sections.title: M&A Appetite
pipeline_sections.topic_template: {buyer_name} has a pattern of acquiring companies in adjacent HR technology markets and would be a natural buyer for an HR media and learning platform

pipeline_sections.title: Competitive Moat
pipeline_sections.topic_template: competitive advantages in HR technology that would be strengthened by acquiring a media and content platform with millions of HR professionals

pipeline_sections.title: Key Earnings Quotes
pipeline_sections.topic_template: {buyer_name} CEO and CFO have discussed acquisition strategy, growth through M&A, and investment in content or media during recent earnings calls

pipeline_sections.title: Approach Strategy
pipeline_sections.topic_template: corporate development and M&A leadership at {buyer_name} who would evaluate an acquisition of an HR media company

pipeline_sections.title: Golden Nuggets
pipeline_sections.topic_template: {buyer_name} executives have made surprising statements about acquiring media businesses, content platforms, or expanding into HR learning and community

pipeline_sections.title: Recent News & Developments
pipeline_sections.topic_template: {buyer_name} has recently announced leadership changes, restructuring, new product launches, or strategic partnerships that would affect their interest in acquiring an HR media company

pipeline_sections.title: Employee Sentiment
pipeline_sections.topic_template: {buyer_name} employees describe their experience working at the company, including culture, leadership, and whether the company is growing or cutting back

pipeline_sections.title: Technology Architecture
pipeline_sections.topic_template: {buyer_name} technology platform, API capabilities, and integration architecture that would determine how easily they could integrate an acquired HR media and learning platform

pipeline_sections.title: Pricing Model & Revenue Mix
pipeline_sections.topic_template: {buyer_name} pricing strategy, subscription model, and revenue breakdown by segment showing how recurring revenue and customer retention drive their business

deal_specific._comment: This section captures HR.com-specific deal structure that does not exist in home_services. It demonstrates where the schema may need extension for non-standard deal types.

asset_structure.notes: Premium 2-letter .com. Domain broker engaged at 10% commission. Can be sold separately; business rebrandable to HR West, HR Certification, or Human Resource Research Institute.
1.2-1.5x revenue. 27 years profitable. ~20% EBITDA. Revenue declining last 3 years (My People focus). 2022 process: 8 offers ($9M-$45M range).
Currently funded at $250K/year by HR.com. Rebuilt 2023, live June 2025. 35 clients interested, 7 going live in 45 days. Needs CEO. TypeScript/Node.js/React, API-first, native AI.

key_relationships: Glenn (Daddy Shark) — Ewing's former boss, Debbie's ski buddy 30+ years
Bill Philip (Delancey and Partners) — brought previous Forge CEO
Doug Merritt — mutual contact with John Kelly, Splunk connection

review_pipeline._comment: HR.com uses a specialized 5-phase buyer research pipeline (debbie_buyer_research.py) that differs from the home_services traced pipeline. These sections define what the Debbie review system produces per buyer.

review_scoring.passing_rule: All three scores must be >= threshold
